headline: Broadcom Joins AI Cybersecurity Drive As Valuation And Growth Draw Focus
summary: Broadcom has joined Project Glasswing, an industry wide cybersecurity initiative focused on using advanced AI models to protect critical software infrastructure. The collaboration brings together Broadcom, Amazon, Anthropic, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia and others to apply AI to vulnerability detection and defensive security tools. The move extends Broadcom's role beyond its core semiconductor and AI chip business into infrastructure software and cybersecurity partnerships. For...

headline: How Can Meta Stock Fall 30%?
summary: Meta Platforms (META) is currently riding a wave of massive ad revenue acceleration. As of early 2026, the company has reported advertising revenue growth in the 22% to 26% range, fueled by sophisticated AI-driven targeting and the aggressive monetization of Reels and WhatsApp. However, market leadership at this scale is difficult to maintain.

content.title: How Can Meta Stock Fall 30%?
content.summary: Meta Platforms (META) is currently riding a wave of massive ad revenue acceleration. As of early 2026, the company has reported advertising revenue growth in the 22% to 26% range, fueled by sophisticated AI-driven targeting and the aggressive monetization of Reels and WhatsApp. However, market leadership at this scale is difficult to maintain.

headline: High Dividends Turn Telecom Stocks Into Market’s New Haven Play
summary: (Bloomberg) -- An uncertain economy and dangerous geopolitical backdrop have investors buying into technology’s most popular haven trade: dividend-rich telecommunications stocks.After a dismal 2025, telecom is one of this year’s top performing sectors in the S&P 500 Index, rising 9.6% while the broad benchmark is down 3.3%. Shares of Verizon Communication Inc. are up more than 19% in 2026, and AT&T Inc. has gained 13%. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq 100 Index fell into a correction in March, and a Bloomb

headline: Gary Black Wants Tesla To Follow Apple's Footsteps To Boost Its FSD Subscriptions
summary: Investor Gary Black of The Future Fund LLC shared on Sunday that he hopes Tesla Inc. would follow Apple Inc.‘s marketing strategy for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system.
